name: Multi-Factor Authentication Bypass
notes:
This rule triggers on login attempts to virtualization or cloud systems without multi-factor authentication (MFA). 

Note: MFA may not be enabled or used in some business scenarios. Thus this rule is disabled by default, and only those environments where MFA is used should enable this rule.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization, BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud
when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Login Success, Authentication.User Login Attempt
when the event matches LOWER("MFA Used") MATCHES '(no|false)' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Multi-Factor Authentication Bypass on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization, BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Login Success, Authentication.User Login Attempt
AND when the event matches LOWER("MFA Used") MATCHES '(no|false)' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects login attempts to virtualization or cloud systems without multi-factor authentication (MFA).
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
